description: FRONTIER: one encoder uses base64url for URL/JWT safety; siblings use standard base64. v0.1 detects only sort-divergence, so it misses this.
inspired_by: Broken links and rejected JWTs from standard base64 in URL positions

findings.rationale: encodeLinkToken uses the url-safe base64url alphabet because its output goes into URLs and JWT segments; its siblings use standard base64 for internal storage. Unify them and link tokens start carrying '+', '/' and '=' — broken URLs and JWT parse failures. An encoding-divergence detector should flag the differing encoding argument; v0.1 (sort-only) cannot.
